name: US 10-Year Treasury Yield
summary: At 4.30% (-2bps Apr 2). UNUSUAL for second consecutive session: yields FELL on peace signal days — should rise when safe-haven demand drops. Bond market shifting from 'inflation worry' to 'recession worry.' Long end falling while short end stable (2Y at 3.61%) = curve pricing GROWTH DETERIORATION, not inflation relief. ISM Prices Index at 78.3 (highest since June 2022, 19.3-point surge in 2 months) with employment in contraction = textbook STAGFLATION. Market pricing 52% probability of Fed HIKE by year-end (first time >50%). Bond-equity divergence is the critical risk signal — when bonds and equities disagree, bonds are usually right.

signals.content: 10Y yield topped 4.06% as surging oil prices raised inflation concerns. Typical safe-haven bond rally did NOT materialize.
Yield rose to 4.37%, highest since July 2025. Market removed every rate cut, pricing in possible hike. 'Pandemonium' in global bond markets.

US 10Y at 4.44% (+10.5% monthly). Oil-driven inflation expectations rising (~40bps in March). Fed trapped in stagflation — can't cut (inflation), can't hike (growth risk). 2Y at 3.61% = 83bps positive slope, but both elevated = stagflation pricing. India 10Y at 6.93% (+6bps) reflecting parallel pressure. ECB froze rate cuts March 19. All three major central banks (Fed, ECB, RBI) on hold simultaneously — global monetary policy paralysis.

US 10Y dropped to 4.34% (-2.2%) — first meaningful yield decline in weeks. 'End war' headlines drove some flight-to-safety unwind. But yields dropping while oil stays elevated is UNUSUAL — signals market shifting from 'inflation' to 'growth/recession' concerns. Stagflation trap: yields can't go much higher (growth risk) or lower (inflation risk). Short-end (2Y at 3.60%) barely moved — curve dynamics. India 10Y GOI at 7.00% (+7bps). CONFIRMED.

US 10Y eased to 4.31% (-0.7%). Pezeshkian peace signal created modest flight-from-safety (yields should RISE when safe-haven demand drops, but they FELL — indicating the market shifted to pricing growth/recession concerns over inflation). DXY dropped below 100 to 99.79. Short-end (2Y at 3.60%) barely moved — curve dynamics suggest long-end pricing growth deterioration while short-end stays anchored on Fed policy. Eurozone inflation surged to 2.5% — energy +4.9% from -3.1%. ECB rate hike speculation emerging. Global stagflation pricing intensifying. India 10Y at 7.03% — breached 7% on record FY27 borrowing. (Sources: yfinance, TradingEconomics, Eurostat)

10Y yield FELL (-2bps to 4.30%) on second consecutive peace signal day — UNUSUAL for second session running. Expected: peace reduces safe-haven demand, yields should rise. Actual: yields fell. Long end falling while short end stable (2Y at 3.61%) = curve pricing GROWTH DETERIORATION, not inflation relief. Bond market has shifted from 'inflation worry' to 'recession worry.' This is the most important divergence in the macro landscape — equities celebrating de-escalation while bonds pricing recession.

ISM Prices Index at 78.3 (highest since June 2022, 19.3-point surge in 2 months). 17 of 18 industries reported higher costs. Employment STILL in contraction despite manufacturing expansion. This is textbook STAGFLATION — can't cut (inflation) and can't hike (growth risk). Market pricing 52% probability of Fed HIKE by year-end (first time >50%). The ISM data validates the bond market's recession signal over the equity market's de-escalation euphoria.

edges.context: 10Y fell to 4.30% — yields dropping for second consecutive peace-signal session, now clearly pricing recession over inflation. ISM Prices 78.3 with employment contracting = stagflation trap. 52% hike probability. Fed completely paralyzed.

Bond-equity divergence: 10Y fell -2bps while S&P rallied +2.9% then gave back -0.52%. Bonds pricing recession, equities pricing de-escalation. When they disagree, bonds usually right. VIX at 24.51 (two sessions below 30).
Higher US yields → capital flows to US → away from EM including India. Contributes to FII outflows. India 10Y at 7.03% — breached 7% first time since July 2024.

trigger_points.mechanism: 5% was the level that crashed markets in Oct 2023. Would signal inflation expectations unanchored. Massive equity selloff risk.
